_instructions_for_ai_fde: 1. Call create_global_branch with: branchName, branchDescription, namespaceRid, ontologyRid below. Capture globalBranchRid AND ontologyBranchRid.

2. For EACH entry in objectTypes (28 total):   a. Call create_object_type_permissions_datasets with {folderRid: saveLocationFolderRid, datasetNames: [<entry>.backingDatasetName]}. Capture the returned dataset RID.  b. In <entry>.createObjectTypePayload, replace ALL occurrences of <entry>.datasetRidPlaceholder with the dataset RID from step 2a, and replace <ONTOLOGY_BRANCH_RID> with the ontologyBranchRid from step 1.  c. Call create_object_type with the substituted payload.
3. After all 28 Object Types are created, call create_or_update_global_branch_proposal with globalBranchRid + proposalTitle + proposalDescription.
4. Report: globalBranchRid, ontologyBranchRid, proposal RID, and the dataset RID + objectType RID for each of the 28 types.
If ANY create_object_type call fails, stop and report the failure with the full tool response.
branchDescription: Initial scale-out of the Argos ontology: 28 Object Types.
proposalDescription: Scale-out of the Argos ontology from object-types.yaml. 28 Object Types covering portfolio, claim, party, financial, evidence, workflow, regulatory, and system-trace layers. Link Types and Action Types follow in separate branches.

createObjectTypePayload.description: TPA client (self-insured, captive, carrier outsource, MGA, or WYO demo branch)

propertyTypes.description: Is Empirically Exercised In V1 (from object-types.yaml).

createObjectTypePayload.description: An insurance policy issued under a client program
A bound effective-dated period of a Policy
A specific coverage (auto BI, flood building, etc.) on a PolicyPeriod
A layer in the coverage tower (primary, SIR, excess, reinsurance, captive)
An insured vehicle or property (v1 = insured assets only)
The underlying event — may spawn multiple Claims across multiple Policies
A claim filed by one insured under one policy from one loss occurrence
The financial and operational pivot — one exposure per claimant per coverage
A person or organization involved in any claim
A role a party holds on a specific claim (parties can hold multiple roles)
A versioned assessment of fault allocation on an exposure
One accounting event; groups one or more balanced FinancialPosting rows
A single signed posting against a named account; balanced under its FinancialTransaction
A recovery opportunity (subro / salvage / contribution / etc.) on an exposure
A document attached to a claim file
Cross-exposure document linkage (a demand package can concern multiple BI exposures)
A specialist's structured read of a document — not source of truth
Canonical event-log row; bitemporal
A scheduled task on an exposure
A request for authority approval (with escalation chain via parent_request_id)
A decision on an authority request
A notice that must be delivered (excess carrier, reinsurer, DOI, etc.)
A lien asserted against an exposure
Shape-only — v1 detects obligation existence; does NOT file Section 111
Per-customer per-specialist configuration (the moat)

A specialist invocation with full audit context. Output adheres to the LegallyBearingClaim contract (AGENT_ARCHITECTURE §3) — every probabilistic claim in output_json must have at least one linked EvidenceCitation row.

A pointer from a probabilistic claim in an AgentAction's output to its supporting source. Every probabilistic claim emitted by a legally-bearing specialist must link to one or more of these (Pydantic min_length=1 contract, AGENT_ARCHITECTURE §3.2). Exactly one of document_id, sourced_rule_id, or ledger_entry_id must be populated.
